rationale: Provides a non-code-specific regulatory framework for human oversight of AI systems that can inform governance of AI-generated code.
Proposes an operationalizable human-in-the-loop and LLM-as-a-judge framework for scaling the evaluation of code patches.
title: Towards a human-in-the-loop framework for reliable patch evaluation using an LLM-as-a-judge

rationale: Synthesizes empirical evidence on developer over-reliance and trust calibration when using AI coding tools, highlighting key oversight challenges.
title: Large language models in programming: a meta-analysis of tools, users, and human-computer interaction themes

rationale: The abstract is thin but suggests a principal-agent governance framework for agentic AI that could inform organizational AI-code oversight.
title: No skin in the game: why agentic AI requires principal-agent governance

rationale: The thin abstract outlines a general authority framework for organizational AI execution that is plausibly transferable to code-generation governance.
title: ECONOMICS OF AUTHORITY EoA #8 federated authority and inter-organizational AI execution the unsolved boundary problem and a dual-authorization mechanism

rationale: Provides only a broad, descriptive overview of AI in software engineering without specific operationalizable frameworks or empirical findings on human oversight.
title: AI in the software development lifecycle: insights and open research questions

rationale: Examines human oversight and trust in a broader machine learning context through XAI, offering transferable insights on decision-making accuracy.
title: Evaluating the impact of explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) techniques on trust, transparency, and decision-making accuracy in high-stakes machine learning applications

rationale: Provides a thin description of a framework for AI integration in knowledge work with human oversight, which may transfer to software engineering.
title: AI will replace functions, not persons: a function-level framework for AI integration in knowledge work

rationale: Focuses entirely on code generation methodologies using modeling languages and lacks any discussion of human oversight or governance.
title: LLM as a code generator in agile model driven development

rationale: Evaluates an LLM's ability to fix its own code smells without addressing human oversight, governance, or organizational scalability.
title: Copilot-in-the-loop: fixing code smells in copilot-generated python code using copilot

rationale: Introduces an intermediate structured representation specifically designed to enable effective human-in-the-loop correction during code generation.
title: A modular code generation method for industrial automation systems based on large-language models

rationale: Discusses organizational governance design and observability for high-speed agentic AI, offering strong theoretical transfer to scaling code oversight.
title: Institutions, organizations, and the bounded agency imperative; rethinking governance design in the age of agentic AI

rationale: Primarily presents an autonomous coding framework and benchmark with basic security sandboxing, rather than addressing the scaling of human oversight.
title: AutoDev: automated AI-driven development

rationale: Presents an operationalizable diagnostic framework for organizational AI governance maturity that can broadly inform AI-code governance strategies.
title: The governance-to-production gap (G2P): a diagnostic model for AI adoption in mid-tier banks

rationale: Provides empirical enterprise data on code review acceptance rates and the shifting role of human oversight in highly AI-augmented developer workflows.
title: One developer is all you need: a case study of an AI-augmented one-person squad in a brownfield enterprise

rationale: Directly maps out concrete governance strategies, review capacity challenges, and accountability mechanisms for AI-generated code within software communities.
title: Beyond banning AI: a first look at GenAI governance in open source software communities

rationale: Focuses entirely on evaluating LLM capabilities for generating test cases locally and lacks any mechanism or discussion related to human oversight.
title: Leveraging pre-trained large language models (LLMs) for on-premises comprehensive automated test case generation: an empirical study

rationale: Explores the scalability limits of human-in-the-loop oversight models in data systems, which is highly transferable to the AI-code volume problem.
title: Lights-off data factory: measuring epistemic autonomy in governance-first data systems

rationale: Directly addresses the concept of vibe coding and explicitly explores the limitations that necessitate governance frameworks for its application.
title: Academic vibe coding: opportunities for accelerating research in an era of resource constraint

rationale: Provides an empirical measurement of license compliance risks in AI-generated code, directly informing organizational risk recognition and governance policies.
title: Licoeval: evaluating LLMs on license compliance in code generation

rationale: Despite a thin abstract, the paper discusses a governance-first approach and NIST risk frameworks that could inform code-generation oversight policies.
title: SOC 2.0: less triage, more treachery

rationale: Explores prompt engineering to reduce vulnerabilities in generated code but does not address organizational governance or human review processes.
title: Balancing security and correctness in code generation: an empirical study on commercial large language models

rationale: Proposes an automated prompt optimization framework without addressing the human oversight, review, or governance of the resulting AI-generated code.
title: REprompt: prompt generation for intelligent software development guided by requirements engineering

rationale: Qualitatively investigates developer over-reliance on AI, the necessity of rigorous human oversight for security, and the need for organizational governance policies.
title: Redefining the programmer: human-AI collaboration, LLMs, and security in modern software engineering

rationale: Empirically evaluates a novel human-in-the-loop mechanism designed to combat developer over-reliance and improve oversight during code generation.
title: HiLDE: intentional code generation via human-in-the-loop decoding
